controls.title: Classification rules for high-risk AI systems
controls.summary: AI systems listed in Annex III or embedded in regulated products (Annex I) are classified as high-risk and must comply with Chapter III requirements. Providers must assess classification before deployment.

controls.title: Risk management system
controls.summary: High-risk AI providers must establish, implement, document, and maintain a risk management system throughout the lifecycle, identifying and mitigating reasonably foreseeable risks.

controls.title: Data and data governance
controls.summary: Training, validation, and testing datasets must meet quality criteria: relevance, representativeness, freedom from errors and completeness. Data governance practices must address biases.

controls.title: Technical documentation
controls.summary: Providers must prepare technical documentation before placing a high-risk AI system on the market, containing information specified in Annex IV, including description, intended purpose, and performance metrics.

controls.title: Record-keeping (automatic logging)
controls.summary: High-risk AI systems must automatically log events ('logs') throughout their lifetime. Logs must be kept for at least six months unless EU or national law prescribes otherwise.

controls.title: Transparency and provision of information to deployers
controls.summary: High-risk AI systems must be designed to allow deployers to understand their operation. Providers must supply instructions for use covering capabilities, limitations, performance, and maintenance.

controls.title: Human oversight
controls.summary: High-risk AI systems must enable natural persons to effectively oversee their operation. Systems must allow override, interruption, and correction. Deployers must assign oversight to competent persons.

controls.title: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
controls.summary: High-risk AI must achieve appropriate levels of accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity resilience against adversarial inputs, data poisoning, and model evasion attacks.

controls.title: Obligations of providers of high-risk AI systems
controls.summary: Providers must: implement the quality management system; draw up technical documentation; apply conformity assessment; register in the EU database; affix CE marking; and comply with post-market monitoring obligations.

controls.title: Quality management system
controls.summary: Providers must implement a quality management system (or equivalent ISO 9001 / ISO 42001) covering risk management, data governance, technical documentation, logging, and corrective action.

controls.title: Obligations of deployers of high-risk AI systems
controls.summary: Deployers must assign human oversight to trained persons, monitor operation, suspend use when risks arise, inform providers of serious incidents, and conduct fundamental rights impact assessments where required.

controls.title: Fundamental rights impact assessment (FRIA)
controls.summary: Before deploying certain high-risk AI (public bodies, financial institutions, insurance, education, employment), a FRIA must be conducted and registered.

controls.title: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems
controls.summary: Providers of AI systems interacting with humans (chatbots), generating synthetic content, or deepfakes must ensure systems disclose their AI nature. Deepfake content must be labelled.

controls.title: Classification of GPAI models
controls.summary: General-purpose AI models with cumulative training compute exceeding 10^25 FLOPs, or posing systemic risk per Commission evaluation, are classified as GPAI models with systemic risk.

controls.title: Obligations for providers of GPAI models
controls.summary: GPAI providers must draw up and maintain technical documentation; provide information to downstream providers; publish a summary of training data; comply with copyright law; and implement an AI policy.

controls.title: Obligations for providers of GPAI models with systemic risk
controls.summary: Systemic-risk GPAI providers must additionally: perform adversarial testing; notify serious incidents to the Commission; ensure cybersecurity protection; and report on energy consumption.

controls.title: Post-market monitoring for high-risk AI
controls.summary: Providers must implement a post-market monitoring plan to collect and review data about performance of their high-risk AI over its lifecycle and address issues proactively.
